checks.label: BLE privacy keywords (heuristic)
checks.description: BLE privacy features reduce tracking.
checks.why: BLE privacy features reduce tracking.
checks.risk_if_fail: If absent, privacy may still be enabled but not shown; treat as unknown.

checks.label: Address rotation toggle (if available)
checks.description: Rotating addresses mitigates persistent tracking.
checks.why: Rotating addresses mitigates persistent tracking.
checks.risk_if_fail: If disabled, long-lived BLE identifiers allow tracking/correlation.

checks.label: Adapter address exposure via settings (should be restricted)
checks.description: Restricting access to the adapter address reduces app-level fingerprinting.
checks.why: Restricting access to the adapter address reduces app-level fingerprinting.
checks.risk_if_fail: If a stable address is exposed, it can be used to correlate the device across contexts.

checks.label: cmd bluetooth_manager dump (inventory)
checks.description: cmd bluetooth_manager may expose structured state useful for auditing.
checks.why: cmd bluetooth_manager may expose structured state useful for auditing.
checks.risk_if_fail: If unavailable, rely on settings/dumpsys checks.

checks.label: cmd bluetooth_manager state snapshot (if supported)
checks.description: cmd bluetooth_manager can provide structured state on some Android versions.
checks.why: cmd bluetooth_manager can provide structured state on some Android versions.
checks.risk_if_fail: If supported, it may reveal additional security-relevant state not visible in settings.
checks.notes: Not all Android versions expose 'cmd bluetooth_manager dump'.

checks.label: BLE scan always available
checks.description: Always-available BLE scanning can undermine user expectations and privacy.
checks.why: Always-available BLE scanning can undermine user expectations and privacy.
checks.risk_if_fail: If enabled, passive BLE collection/tracking can occur even when the user thinks BT is off.

checks.label: Bluetooth toggle state
checks.description: Minimize radio exposure by keeping Bluetooth disabled when not needed.
checks.why: Minimize radio exposure by keeping Bluetooth disabled when not needed.
checks.risk_if_fail: If Bluetooth is ON continuously, the device is always reachable over a proximity channel.

checks.label: Developer options enabled (risk surface)
checks.description: Developer options often expose diagnostic toggles (including HCI snoop logging) and weaken hardening.
checks.why: Developer options often expose diagnostic toggles (including HCI snoop logging) and weaken hardening.
checks.risk_if_fail: If enabled, misconfiguration risk rises (debug logs, test toggles, etc.).

checks.label: Global Bluetooth-related settings inventory
checks.description: Inventorying settings keys helps detect OEM knobs that override UI expectations.
checks.why: Inventorying settings keys helps detect OEM knobs that override UI expectations.
checks.risk_if_fail: Hidden keys may keep BT discoverable, scanning, or logging enabled.

checks.label: Secure Bluetooth-related settings inventory
checks.description: Secure settings commonly store HCI snoop and adapter identity keys.
checks.why: Secure settings commonly store HCI snoop and adapter identity keys.
checks.risk_if_fail: Mis-set secure keys can enable sensitive logging.

checks.label: Third-party Bluetooth packages (non-system)
checks.description: Third-party BT apps can increase attack surface.
checks.why: Third-party BT apps can increase attack surface.
checks.risk_if_fail: Overprivileged or malicious BT-related apps can abuse the BT subsystem.

checks.label: Disabled Bluetooth packages present
checks.description: Unexpectedly disabled BT packages can indicate tampering or broken policy.
checks.why: Unexpectedly disabled BT packages can indicate tampering or broken policy.
checks.risk_if_fail: If core components are disabled, device may behave unpredictably or rely on vendor replacements.

checks.label: List Bluetooth-related packages
checks.description: Inventory of BT packages helps detect overlays/replacements.
checks.why: Inventory of BT packages helps detect overlays/replacements.
checks.risk_if_fail: Unexpected packages may add attack surface or intercept BT operations.

checks.label: Path of core BT package (com.android.bluetooth)
checks.description: Validating the install path helps ensure the BT stack is system-managed.
checks.why: Validating the install path helps ensure the BT stack is system-managed.
checks.risk_if_fail: If path is absent or non-system, it may indicate replacement/tampering.
checks.expected_secure_state: Must resolve to a system path

checks.label: Bluetooth library name
checks.description: Library identifier helps correlate OEM components.
checks.why: Library identifier helps correlate OEM components.
checks.risk_if_fail: Unexpected values may require supply-chain review.

checks.label: Bluetooth stack property
checks.description: Stack identifier supports triage (Fluoride/GD vs legacy).
checks.why: Stack identifier supports triage (Fluoride/GD vs legacy).
checks.risk_if_fail: Legacy stacks can have more historical CVE exposure.

checks.label: Build type is production
checks.description: Production builds typically ship hardened and are expected in operational environments.
checks.why: Production builds typically ship hardened and are expected in operational environments.
checks.risk_if_fail: Debug builds can enable additional interfaces and weaken security posture.

checks.label: Android version (inventory)
checks.description: OS version influences which Bluetooth security features exist.
checks.why: OS version influences which Bluetooth security features exist.
checks.risk_if_fail: Older OS versions may lack modern Bluetooth hardening.

checks.label: Security patch level
checks.description: Patch level is required for vulnerability management of known Bluetooth CVEs.
checks.why: Patch level is required for vulnerability management of known Bluetooth CVEs.
checks.risk_if_fail: Old patches correlate strongly with known exploitable Bluetooth issues.

checks.label: Bluetooth-related properties snapshot
checks.description: Property inventory can reveal debug/logging/test toggles.
checks.why: Property inventory can reveal debug/logging/test toggles.
checks.risk_if_fail: Mis-set properties can silently reduce security.

checks.label: Bonded devices listing
checks.description: Auditing bonded devices ensures only approved peripherals retain trust.
checks.why: Auditing bonded devices ensures only approved peripherals retain trust.
checks.risk_if_fail: Unknown bonds can silently reconnect and access sensitive services.

checks.label: Bonded devices listing
checks.description: NIST recommends auditing paired devices and removing unauthorized or stale trust relationships.
checks.why: NIST recommends auditing paired devices and removing unauthorized or stale trust relationships.
checks.risk_if_fail: Unknown bonded devices can silently reconnect and access data/services permitted by profiles.
checks.notes: Implement your own allowlist matching (MAC/name) on the host side to auto-flag unknown bonds.
checks.expected_secure_state: Only authorized devices bonded (prefer minimal list)

checks.label: Count bonded devices (heuristic)
checks.command: dumpsys bluetooth_manager | grep -A 80 -i 'Bonded devices' | grep -iE 'Device|address|name' | wc -l
checks.description: A smaller bonded list reduces persistent trust and reconnection risk.
checks.why: A smaller bonded list reduces persistent trust and reconnection risk.
checks.risk_if_fail: Large numbers of bonds increase the likelihood of stale/compromised devices remaining trusted.
checks.notes: This count is noisy across OEMs; treat as trend indicator not exact count.
checks.expected_secure_state: 0 is best for high-security devices; otherwise keep minimal

checks.label: Bond state keywords (inventory)
checks.description: Bond state lines can reveal unexpected bonding activity.
checks.why: Bond state lines can reveal unexpected bonding activity.
checks.risk_if_fail: Unexpected bonding can indicate user confusion or malicious pairing attempts.

checks.label: Adapter scan mode
checks.description: Discoverability should be OFF by default and used only briefly for pairing.
checks.why: Discoverability should be OFF by default and used only briefly for pairing.
checks.risk_if_fail: If CONNECTABLE_DISCOVERABLE, attackers can easily find and target the device.

checks.label: Discoverable timeout (if reported)
checks.description: Timeout limits exposure window while discoverable.
checks.why: Timeout limits exposure window while discoverable.
checks.risk_if_fail: Long/indefinite discoverability increases enumeration and tracking risk.
checks.notes: Field varies by Android/OEM; if absent, rely on BT-010.

checks.label: Discoverability settings keys (OEM-dependent inventory)
checks.description: Some OEMs expose discoverability toggles via global settings keys.
checks.why: Some OEMs expose discoverability toggles via global settings keys.
checks.risk_if_fail: If present and set incorrectly, device may remain discoverable.

checks.label: Active connections encrypted (generic)
checks.description: NIST recommends encryption to protect confidentiality of Bluetooth communications.
checks.why: NIST recommends encryption to protect confidentiality of Bluetooth communications.
checks.risk_if_fail: Unencrypted links allow passive sniffing and data recovery within radio range.
checks.notes: Many devices only show encryption flags when a connection is active.

checks.label: Encryption flags (generic)
checks.description: Encryption is required to protect confidentiality of Bluetooth communications.
checks.why: Encryption is required to protect confidentiality of Bluetooth communications.
checks.risk_if_fail: Unencrypted sessions can be passively captured and decoded.
checks.notes: Most useful when a connection is active.

checks.label: Encryption key length (KNOB risk heuristic)
checks.description: Key length negotiation should not be downgraded.
checks.why: Key length negotiation should not be downgraded.
checks.risk_if_fail: Weak key lengths can enable brute-force decryption (e.g., KNOB-style entropy reduction on vulnerable stacks).
checks.notes: If key length isn't printed, validate via patch level and SC enforcement.
checks.expected_secure_state: Key length should indicate 16 octets / 128-bit when reported

checks.label: Encryption key length (if exposed)
checks.description: Strong keys resist brute-force decryption.
checks.why: Strong keys resist brute-force decryption.
checks.risk_if_fail: Weak/negotiated-down key length enables traffic decryption (e.g., KNOB-style risks on vulnerable stacks).

checks.label: Profile keywords present: HFP/HSP (Handsfree)
checks.description: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.why: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.risk_if_fail: Extra profiles can expose sensitive data/services to paired devices.
checks.notes: If output is non-empty, confirm whether profile is enabled/connected and whether it is required.
checks.expected_secure_state: Unused profiles should be disabled; if present, must be justified

checks.label: Profile keywords present: HID (Input)
checks.description: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.why: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.risk_if_fail: Extra profiles can expose sensitive data/services to paired devices.
checks.notes: If output is non-empty, confirm whether profile is enabled/connected and whether it is required.
checks.expected_secure_state: Unused profiles should be disabled; if present, must be justified

checks.label: Profile keywords present: MAP (Message Access)
checks.description: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.why: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.risk_if_fail: Extra profiles can expose sensitive data/services to paired devices.
checks.notes: If output is non-empty, confirm whether profile is enabled/connected and whether it is required.
checks.expected_secure_state: Unused profiles should be disabled; if present, must be justified

checks.label: Profile keywords present: OPP (Object Push)
checks.description: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.why: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.risk_if_fail: Extra profiles can expose sensitive data/services to paired devices.
checks.notes: If output is non-empty, confirm whether profile is enabled/connected and whether it is required.
checks.expected_secure_state: Unused profiles should be disabled; if present, must be justified

checks.label: Profile keywords present: PAN (Network)
checks.description: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.why: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.risk_if_fail: Extra profiles can expose sensitive data/services to paired devices.
checks.notes: If output is non-empty, confirm whether profile is enabled/connected and whether it is required.
checks.expected_secure_state: Unused profiles should be disabled; if present, must be justified

checks.label: Profile keywords present: PBAP (Phonebook)
checks.description: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.why: Disabling unused profiles reduces attack surface.
checks.risk_if_fail: Extra profiles can expose sensitive data/services to paired devices.
checks.notes: If output is non-empty, confirm whether profile is enabled/connected and whether it is required.
checks.expected_secure_state: Unused profiles should be disabled; if present, must be justified

checks.label: All profile/service lines (inventory)
checks.description: A profile inventory helps validate whether only required services are exposed.
checks.why: A profile inventory helps validate whether only required services are exposed.
checks.risk_if_fail: Unexpected services can indicate OEM additions or extra attack surface.

checks.label: dumpsys snoop indicators
checks.description: Some builds include runtime snoop mode info in dumpsys.
checks.why: Some builds include runtime snoop mode info in dumpsys.
checks.risk_if_fail: If enabled, debugging capture may expose sensitive artifacts.

checks.label: Snoop-related properties snapshot
checks.description: OEMs sometimes control logging via properties.
checks.why: OEMs sometimes control logging via properties.
checks.risk_if_fail: Forced-on logging can persist across reboots.

checks.label: Secure setting bluetooth_hci_log
checks.description: HCI snoop logging is a debug feature and should be disabled outside troubleshooting.
checks.why: HCI snoop logging is a debug feature and should be disabled outside troubleshooting.
checks.risk_if_fail: If enabled, sensitive Bluetooth traffic/metadata may be written and later exfiltrated.

checks.label: Boot SELinux mode property
checks.description: SELinux confinement is part of platform hardening for system services.
checks.why: SELinux confinement is part of platform hardening for system services.
checks.risk_if_fail: Permissive/disabled SELinux increases blast radius of Bluetooth stack vulnerabilities.
checks.notes: If absent, treat as unknown under these command constraints.

checks.label: Device state (bootloader lock signal)
checks.description: A locked device state supports integrity of platform security controls.
checks.why: A locked device state supports integrity of platform security controls.
checks.risk_if_fail: Unlocked state increases tampering risk for BT stack/policies.

checks.label: Verified Boot state
checks.description: Verified Boot provides confidence that system components (including BT stack) are not modified.
checks.why: Verified Boot provides confidence that system components (including BT stack) are not modified.
checks.risk_if_fail: Non-green states indicate a modified/untrusted system image.

checks.label: Just Works usage indicators
checks.description: Just Works provides no MITM protection.
checks.why: Just Works provides no MITM protection.
checks.risk_if_fail: If used, an active attacker can MITM the pairing/association.

checks.label: Secure Connections (SC) indicators
checks.description: Secure Connections strengthens pairing/key agreement.
checks.why: Secure Connections strengthens pairing/key agreement.
checks.risk_if_fail: If SC is absent/disabled, device may accept legacy association and weaker keying.

checks.label: SSP indicators in stack dump
checks.description: SSP provides stronger association than legacy PIN pairing.
checks.why: SSP provides stronger association than legacy PIN pairing.
checks.risk_if_fail: If SSP is disabled/downgraded, pairing is more MITM/bruteforce-prone.
checks.notes: Absence doesn't prove SSP is off; output is OEM-dependent.

checks.label: SELinux Bluetooth Domain Policy
checks.description: The Bluetooth binary should be labeled 'bluetooth:s0' (restrictive domain); otherwise SELinux policy may be weakened.

checks.label: Bluetooth Daemon Hardening
checks.command: (ps -A -o cmd 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'bluetoothd|bluetooth' | grep -q -- '-n') && echo HARDENED || echo CHECK_REQUIRED
checks.description: The Bluetooth daemon should run with the '-n' flag (no‑privilege mode) to prevent loading unnecessary plugins.

checks.label: bluetoothd Hardening
checks.command: (ps -A -o cmd 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'bluetoothd|bluetooth' | grep -q -- -n) && echo HARDENED || echo UNPROTECTED
checks.description: Checks if the Bluetooth daemon runs in hardened (no-privilege) mode.

checks.label: Airplane Mode Enforces BT Off
checks.command: APM=$(settings get global airplane_mode_on); BT=$(settings get global bluetooth_on); echo "AirplaneMode=$APM BT=$BT"
checks.description: When airplane mode is on, Bluetooth must also be off to prevent RF emissions in restricted environments.

checks.label: BLE MAC Randomization Status
checks.command: VAR=$(settings get global bluetooth_address_rotation_enabled 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$VAR" ] || [ "$VAR" = "null" ]; then echo NotSupported; elif [ "$VAR" = "1" ]; then echo Enabled; else echo Disabled; fi
checks.description: BLE MAC randomization must be enabled to prevent persistent device tracking.

checks.label: BLE MAC Randomization
checks.command: VAR=$(settings get global bluetooth_address_rotation_enabled 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$VAR" ]; then echo NotSupported; elif [ "$VAR" = "1" ]; then echo Enabled; else echo Disabled; fi
checks.description: Should be enabled to randomize BLE MAC (prevents device tracking/leakage).

checks.label: Bluetooth Stack (Not Bluedroid)
checks.command: [ $(getprop ro.bluetooth.stack 2>/dev/null | grep -i bluedroid | wc -l) -gt 0 ] && echo VULNERABLE || echo SAFE
checks.description: The Bluetooth stack should not be Bluedroid (vulnerable to BlueBorne). Fluoride is the modern, hardened replacement.

checks.label: Active Connection Encryption
checks.command: active=$(dumpsys bluetooth_manager 2>/dev/null | grep -A 5 'Active Devices:' | grep 'Encrypted' | awk '{print $2}'); if [ -z "$active" ] || echo "$active" | grep -q 'true'; then echo PASS; else echo FAIL; fi
checks.description: All active Bluetooth connections must be encrypted (NIST SC-13).

checks.label: Secure Simple Pairing (SSP) Enabled
checks.command: dumpsys bluetooth_manager 2>/dev/null | grep -iq 'Secure Simple Pairing.*Enabled' && echo Enabled || echo Disabled
checks.description: SSP must be enabled; otherwise legacy pairing is vulnerable to PIN brute‑force and KNOB downgrade attacks.

checks.label: Authentication Required
checks.description: Authentication must be required for pairing (IA-2)

checks.label: Paired Devices Count
checks.command: dumpsys bluetooth_manager | grep 'Bonded devices:' -A 10 | grep 'Device:' | wc -l
checks.description: Review all paired devices (AC-3)

checks.label: Bluetooth Encryption
checks.description: Bluetooth encryption must be enabled (SC-13)

checks.label: LE Security Mode
checks.description: LE should use Mode 2 (Secure Connections) or higher (SC-13)

checks.label: Secure Pairing Mode
checks.description: Only secure pairing modes should be allowed (NIST IA-2)

checks.label: Unnecessary Profiles
checks.description: Disable unused profiles (CM-7)

checks.label: Paired Device Count
checks.command: dumpsys bluetooth_manager | grep -A 10 'Bonded devices:' | grep 'Device:' | wc -l
checks.description: The number of paired devices should be zero; each pair is a persistent trust and potential attack vector.

checks.label: Bonded/Paired Device Audit
checks.description: Manually review bonded devices. Any unknown MAC indicates unauthorized pairing or a silent backdoor.

checks.label: Legacy Bluetooth Profiles
checks.description: Legacy profiles can expose device to known CVEs—disable if not needed.

checks.label: Authentication Required
checks.description: Authentication must be required for pairing to prevent BIAS attacks (CVE-2020-10135).

checks.label: Bluetooth Debugging Interface
checks.description: Bluetooth debugging interfaces must be disabled in production (CM-6)

checks.label: Link Key Strength
checks.description: Link encryption key length must be at least 128 bits (IA-5)

checks.label: Link Key Strength (128-bit)
checks.description: Link encryption keys must be at least 128 bits; shorter keys are vulnerable to the KNOB attack.

checks.label: LE Security Mode
checks.description: LE Security Mode must be 2, 3, or 4; Mode 1 or absence enables the BLESA attack (CVE-2020-9770).

checks.label: Exposed Profile: HID (Input Device)
checks.description: HID should not be connected; otherwise keystroke injection and BlueBorne RCE (CVE-2017-0785) are possible.

checks.label: I/O Capability (Not NoInputNoOutput)
checks.description: I/O capability should allow MITM protection (e.g., DisplayYesNo). NoInputNoOutput forces JustWorks and is insecure.

checks.label: Exposed Profile: MAP (Message Access)
checks.description: MAP should be disconnected (mState: 0); otherwise SMS and 2FA codes are remotely readable.

checks.label: Exposed Profile: OPP (File Transfer)
checks.description: OPP should be disconnected or not present; otherwise unsolicited file pushes (CVE-2018-9426) are possible.

checks.label: Exposed Profile: PBAP (Phonebook)
checks.description: PBAP should be disconnected; otherwise the full contacts database can be exfiltrated silently.

checks.label: Exposed Profile: SAP (SIM Access)
checks.description: SAP should be disconnected; otherwise a remote device can fully control the SIM card.

checks.label: BLE UUID/Service Advertisement
checks.description: No active BLE advertisements should be present; they leak device fingerprints even with MAC randomization.

checks.label: Encryption on Active Connections
checks.description: All active Bluetooth connections must be encrypted; otherwise raw HCI payload can be captured by passive radio sniffing.

checks.label: Exposed Profile: PAN (Network Access)
checks.description: PAN tethering (mTetherOn) must be false to prevent unauthorized network access and traffic interception.

checks.label: Bluetooth Stack Version
checks.description: Check the Bluetooth stack version against the latest for your Android version; outdated versions may contain known CVEs.

checks.label: Apps with BLUETOOTH_CONNECT Permission
checks.command: dumpsys package | grep -B5 'BLUETOOTH_CONNECT' | grep 'packageName' | sort -u | wc -l
checks.description: Fewer than six apps should hold BLUETOOTH_CONNECT; over‑permissioned apps can expose MACs and connection states.

checks.label: SELinux Enforcement Status
checks.description: SELinux must be Enforcing; Permissive or Disabled allows unrestricted Bluetooth daemon activity and privilege escalation.

checks.label: bluetooth.opp Property
checks.description: OPP (Object Push Profile) should be disabled if not needed.

checks.label: bluetooth.pan Property
checks.description: PAN (Personal Area Networking) profile should be disabled if not needed.

checks.label: bluetooth.pbap Property
checks.description: PBAP (Phone Book Access Profile) should be disabled if not needed.

checks.label: bluetooth.sap Property
checks.description: SAP (SIM Access Profile) should be disabled if not needed.

checks.label: bluetooth.secure Property
checks.description: Bluetooth secure mode should be enabled (property).

checks.label: Bluetooth Version ≥ 5.0
checks.description: Bluetooth version should be 5.0 or newer to support LE Secure Connections and mitigate SweynTooth/BLESA.

checks.label: Bluetooth Debug Properties
checks.description: Debug properties for Bluetooth should not be set on production.

checks.label: Bluetooth Debug Properties
checks.description: No debug-related Bluetooth properties should be set; they disable security checks and expose test interfaces.

checks.label: Bluetooth Stack Crash Log
checks.description: No recent Bluetooth crashes in the log; crashes may indicate exploitation attempts like BlueFrag (CVE-2020-0022).

checks.label: Raw HCI Device Nodes Unexposed
checks.description: Presence of /dev/hci* or /dev/radio* nodes allows direct HCI command injection, bypassing Android's security layer.

checks.label: Bluetooth Firmware Present
checks.description: At least one Bluetooth firmware file should exist; absence may indicate tampering or custom radio firmware.

checks.label: Custom Vendor BT Libraries
checks.description: At least one vendor Bluetooth HAL library should exist; absence or unexpected files may indicate tampering or supply chain risk.

checks.label: Apps with BLUETOOTH Permission
checks.description: Too many apps with BLUETOOTH permission increases risk.

checks.label: Apps with BLUETOOTH_ADMIN Permission
checks.description: Fewer than three apps should hold BLUETOOTH_ADMIN; excess apps can toggle Bluetooth, clear bonds, and change scan mode.

checks.label: Auto‑enable Bluetooth
checks.description: Bluetooth should not automatically turn on (NIST AC‑18)

checks.label: BR/EDR Secure Connections Enforcement
checks.command: settings get global bluetooth_br_edr_secure_connections 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^1$' && echo Enforced || echo NotEnforced
checks.description: BR/EDR Secure Connections must be enforced to prevent the KNOB attack (CVE-2019-9506).

checks.label: Discoverable Mode
checks.description: Device should not be discoverable (AC-18)

checks.label: MAC Randomization
checks.description: Static MAC addresses should be avoided (SC-8(1))

checks.label: BT Device Name Info Leakage
checks.description: Bluetooth device name should be null or generic; names revealing model, owner, or organization aid OSINT and targeted attacks.
